description_public: A polygenic estimate related to alcohol flush reaction tendency variation in the general population.

GG.notes: This genotype is associated with typical ALDH2 enzyme activity, so there is less tendency toward facial flushing from small amounts of alcohol, although individual sensitivity still varies.

GA.notes: This genotype is associated with reduced ALDH2 activity and a higher chance of facial flushing, nausea or discomfort even with modest alcohol intake. It is also linked with higher acetaldehyde buildup, so minimizing alcohol use is especially important.

AA.notes: This genotype is associated with very low ALDH2 activity and a strong tendency to flush and feel unwell with alcohol. This is informational only and not medical advice, but it supports being very cautious with drinking.

evidence.title: Placeholder evidence for Alcohol flush reaction tendency
evidence.quote: This genotype is associated with typical ALDH2 enzyme activity, so there is less tendency toward facial flushing from small amounts of alcohol, although individual sensitivity still varies.
